# Book 1 — Prose Realism Quality Report (Sample)

This document is a **representative** Cluster 5 report illustrating how realism diagnostics read after a chapter-scale run. Figures are **illustrative**; live runs bind to stored governance packs and generated scene text.

## Multi-scene chapter snapshot

| Scene | Inferred role | Narrator boundary focus | Era/cognition note |
|-------|----------------|---------------------------|--------------------|
| 1 | Grounding | Witness-heavy; low interpretive stack | Material labor + river humidity |
| 2 | Pressure | Relational stakes in behavior | No modern affect labels |
| 3 | Closure | Non-reset ending preferred | Consequence shadow via silence/proximity |

## Scene-by-scene diagnostics (pattern)

- **Realism score**: blended 0–1 across ten dimensions (see `ProseRealismProfile`).
- **Hard failures**: modern cognition leak, omniscient intrusion, system-language leak.
- **Soft warnings**: low sensory grounding, emotional overstatement, repeated sentence openers.

## Narrator vs character (example handling)

- **Framing lane**: “The room judged her before anyone spoke” — social inference, not clinical interiority.
- **Character-close**: “She kept her hands busy so her face would not have to answer” — action routing, era-plausible.

## Era-truth (example)

- **Avoid**: “trauma response,” “processed her feelings,” “mindfulness.”
- **Prefer**: fatigue, vigilance, shame as **witnessed** social cost and body cost.

## Emotional residue / carry-forward (example)

- End on **unfinished** proximity or withheld speech when EEGS flags reset-heavy risk.
- Let **fear/desire lines** from the gravity pack appear as **timing** and **route** choices, not as named emotions.

## Literary naturalization (example)

- Alliteration confined to labor chorus or wind/water texture—not tongue-twister ornament.
- Symbol binds to **kin object** or **route memory** already in the contract.

## Remaining weak spots (typical)

- Thin place load when scene order is synthetic in DB-only governance adapter.
- First-person readiness gated—do not force first person when convergence flags warn.

## Next hardening

- Per-scene **callback phrase** half-echo detection in validation (deterministic).
- Optional **cross-scene cadence** comparison when multiple scenes generated in one chapter batch.
